title: OCDE: el crecimiento mundial se reducirá drásticamente si la guerra se alarga hasta 2027
summary: La Organización alerta de que si el conflicto no se resuelve a corto plazo, el PIB mundial vería mermado su crecimiento hasta el 2,1% en 2026, frente al 3,4% de 2025, y al 1,8% en 2027, con más inflación y paro, menos inversión e incluso recesión en "algunas economías". Leer

summary: El sistema representa la vanguardia de la inteligencia de datos aplicada a la detección del fraude. La Agencia Tributaria española ha adquirido licencias para explotar cuatro aplicaciones del sistema. Leer

title: El Ibex agradece el tirón de Inditex
summary: Los mercados optan por la cautela ante el nuevo paso adelante que registra el petróleo, impulsado por la falta de avances en las negociaciones con Irán. El Ibex cuenta con su propia referencia de peso, los resultados de su mayor empresa, Inditex. Leer

title: Quién va a 'comerse' el aluvión de 1 billón de euros en acciones que llega a Bolsa
summary: Cómo medir el riesgo de que el mercado se atragante. Leer

title: Sánchez se compromete ante el empresariado catalán a culminar sus pactos con Junts y ERC
summary: Defiende sus pactos con partidos nacionalistas e independentistas: "Están sentando bien al conjunto del Estado y ahí están las cifras económicas". Leer

title: Correos se transforma en la gran ventanilla del Estado: venderá deuda pública y expedirá certificados digitales
summary: El Gobierno aprueba un contrato-programa hasta 2030 que garantiza 400 millones de euros iniciales para blindar el servicio postal y desarrollar nuevas prestaciones. Leer

title: New EU law forces employers in Spain to publish salary before job interview
summary: A new European Union directive on pay transparency means that companies in Spain must provide more information on salaries in the job advert itself and before the application and interview process.

title: Mercadona to open on Sundays over summer in Spain's tourist areas
summary: Spain's most popular supermarket has announced that it will be open for longer in areas of high-tourist concentration during the peak summer months, closing later on weekdays and opening on Sundays.

title: Migrant boat crossings in focus ahead of Pope's visit to Spain's Canaries
summary: Since Pope Leo XIV’s visit to Spain was announced, the pontiff voiced his intent to stop in the Canary Islands to highlight the humanitarian crisis on the deadly Atlantic migration route, as well as to meet with migrants, rescue workers, and aid organisations.

title: Spanish mayor bans DR Congo pre-World Cup game due to Ebola fears
summary: A pre-World Cup friendly involving the Democratic Republic of Congo has been cancelled by the mayor of the Spanish town hosting the match over health concerns regarding the Ebola outbreak in the African country.

title: Spain's longest suspension bridge to open at Málaga's Caminito del Rey
summary: What was once considered 'the world's most dangerous walkway' is getting a new alternative route in the form of a 110-metre-long suspension bridge, an alternative route for hikers on this iconic trekking spot in southern Spain.

title: EU calls on nine European countries to scrap internal Schengen border checks
summary: The European Commission on Tuesday asked nine countries in the Schengen area, including France, Germany, Sweden, Austria, Italy, Denmark and Norway to phase out internal border checks, which have been reinstated in some cases for years.

title: Spain stands firm as only EU country to oppose new deportation centre deal
summary: The EU reached a deal Monday on a new tightening of migration rules allowing for so-called "return hubs" to be set up outside the bloc, as countries seek to boost deportations. Spain is the only EU country to vote against this measure.

title: How to get involved with urban gardens in Spain
summary: If you fancy yourself green-fingered or live in an apartment without access to your own outdoor space, you'll find that Spain has many urban gardens and allotments that you can potentially join.
